title: cynara communis
intro: Cynara communis, also known as artichoke, is a perennial thistle-like plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's edible flower buds have been used for medicinal purposes, boasting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Consuming cynara communis has been linked to various health benefits, including improved digestion, lower cholesterol levels, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. Artichoke leaves and flowers are often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support liver health and promote overall well-being.
